description: Eliminate the 'slot 1 merge-back failed because slot 0's working tree was dirty' class of failure in the parallel-slot loop. Primary fix: gitignore the per-PRD progress files (`tasks/progress-*.txt`) so they never participate in merge-back preconditions or cleanup-dirty checks. Defense-in-depth: stash-based preflight on the per-slot merge for any residual non-progress dirty state (build artifacts, log files), bounded warn-and-continue on pop conflicts (configurable via ProjectConfig.slot_stash_limit), and auto-recovery in reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots for legacy strandings created before this PRD landed.

criteria: Rust: no warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
Rust: scoped tests pass (`cargo test -p task-mgr <module>::` or narrower)

No breaking changes to existing public APIs unless the task description explicitly authorizes it (signature changes on `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` and `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots` ARE authorized — every caller must be updated in the same task)
No unwrap() in production code paths; use map_err with TaskMgrError variants
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Auto-commits to the base branch (chore(progress) or similar) — the whole point of the gitignore is to keep progress files out of git entirely
Stashes that leak across iterations because cleanup was skipped on an exit path
Bare git stash@{N} positional refs in production code — always look up by tag
A migration step that does `git rm` (non-cached) and deletes the operator's existing progress data — only `git rm --cached` is allowed; the file content must survive on disk

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: after `task-mgr init` on a fresh repo, `.gitignore` exists and contains a marker block delimiting `tasks/progress-*.txt`
Positive: re-running `task-mgr init` is a byte-for-byte no-op on `.gitignore` (no diff produced)
Positive: if `.gitignore` had pre-existing content, it survives the marker-block insertion verbatim
Unit test: marker-block rewrite when the body drifted from prior version (mirror test_merged_attributes_rewrites_drifted_block at worktree.rs ~line 2152)
Unit test: appends to existing .gitignore with one blank-line separator
Unit test: idempotent re-run when block already matches → no rewrite
Unit test: `git rm --cached` runs for each match from `git ls-files tasks/progress-*.txt`; the file content on disk is unchanged (read-after-migration shows the original content)
Unit test: migration is a no-op (no commit) when `git ls-files tasks/progress-*.txt` returns empty
Unit test: migration creates exactly one commit with message starting with `chore: untrack progress files` when at least one file was tracked
Negative: `git rm` (non-cached) is NEVER invoked — verified by grep on the implementation file
Negative: the gitignore entry pattern is `tasks/progress-*.txt`, NOT `tasks/` or `*.txt` (assert exact pattern in test)
Negative: no migration commit on a branch other than HEAD
Failure mode: `git ls-files` fails (e.g., not a git repo) → migration step skipped with stderr warning; .gitignore step still completes
Failure mode: `git commit` fails (e.g., pre-commit hook rejects) → surface error to user; .gitignore changes already on disk should be left for manual recovery
userStories.description:
Eliminate the most common merge-back precondition failure by ensuring per-PRD progress files (`tasks/progress-*.txt`) are NEVER tracked by git. Both the loop engine's progress logging and the agent's progress appends will then accumulate in untracked files that git's merge-precondition + dirty-worktree checks ignore entirely.

DO:
- During `task-mgr init` (and the `task-mgr loop init` / `task-mgr batch init` paths that call init_project), ensure a `.gitignore` entry exists for `tasks/progress-*.txt`. Idempotent: if the entry is already present, no-op. If `.gitignore` doesn't exist, create it with the single entry (don't dump a big default gitignore).
- Use a marker-delimited block like the `ensure_progress_union_merge` pattern (worktree.rs:424-434): `# task-mgr begin: progress files (untracked)` / `tasks/progress-*.txt` / `# task-mgr end: progress files (untracked)`. Content outside the markers is preserved untouched.
- One-time migration: at the same call site, run `git ls-files tasks/progress-*.txt`. For each match, run `git rm --cached <path>` (NOT `git rm` — the file content must stay on disk for the operator to keep their history visible). Then `git commit -m "chore: untrack progress files (task-mgr init migration)"` on the current branch ONLY IF there were matches. If there were no matches, no commit.
- Add unit tests verifying: fresh repo gets the marker block, existing .gitignore is preserved + appended, idempotent re-run produces no diff, migration runs `git rm --cached` only when files are tracked, migration is a no-op when nothing is tracked.

DO NOT:
- Delete the progress file from disk. `git rm --cached` is the ONLY allowed migration. The operator's progress history is content they may want to read.
- Touch the loop's per-iteration progress writing logic in this task. The writing path (iteration_pipeline.rs::progress_path, branch.rs::progress_file_name) keeps writing to `tasks/progress-{prefix}.txt` per-worktree — git just doesn't see those files anymore.
- Add an entry that's broader than `tasks/progress-*.txt` (e.g., the bare `tasks/` or `*.txt`). The pattern must be tight enough to avoid accidentally ignoring task-list JSON or learnings.
- Run the migration commit on a different branch than the one `task-mgr init` is invoked on.

Edge cases:
- `.gitignore` doesn't exist → create with marker block + the one entry.
- `.gitignore` exists but doesn't have the marker → append marker block with one blank-line separator.
- `.gitignore` has the marker block with the right body → no-op.
- `.gitignore` has the marker block but body drifted from prior version → rewrite the body in place (mirrors `merged_attributes_contents` in worktree.rs:467).
- No tracked progress files → no migration commit, no error.
- Tracked progress files exist on a dirty worktree → still safe (`git rm --cached` doesn't touch WT); commit message clearly attributes the cache-only removal.
- Bare repo / no commits yet → migration step is a no-op (nothing to ls-files); .gitignore still gets written.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Calling `git rm` without `--cached` would delete the operator's progress files from disk — irreversible data loss. The test must include a 'progress file content survives migration' assertion.
- Appending the pattern at the end without a marker block (just `echo '...' >> .gitignore`) would prevent idempotent re-runs from detecting the existing entry and would duplicate the line on each `task-mgr init`. Use the marker block.
edgeCases: .gitignore missing → created with marker block
.gitignore exists with unrelated content → marker block appended, original preserved
Marker block exists with drifted body → body rewritten, surrounding preserved
Marker block exists with matching body → no-op
No tracked progress files → migration no-op, no commit
Tracked progress files on dirty WT → cached removal safe; commit succeeds
Not a git repo → .gitignore still written; migration step skipped with warning

failureModes.expectedBehavior: migration skipped with stderr warning; .gitignore write still proceeds
surface error to user; .gitignore write is already committed to disk (not the index) and survives for manual cleanup
return TaskMgrError::IoError with context; do not partially write

userStories.notes: Init code lives in src/commands/init/. Look for the `init_project` function entry point. Use the marker-block pattern from worktree.rs:424-505 (`merged_attributes_contents`, `ensure_progress_union_merge`) — same shape, different file (`.gitignore` instead of `info/attributes`), different body. Run `git ls-files tasks/progress-*.txt 2>/dev/null` to enumerate tracked files. `git rm --cached` does not touch WT; safe even if WT is dirty. The migration commit should be on the current branch — use `git symbolic-ref HEAD` or `git branch --show-current` to confirm. Learning [1825]: slot-aware patterns already exist in worktree.rs; mirror them rather than inventing.
qualityDimensions: Marker-block rewrite is idempotent and preserves user-authored gitignore lines verbatim
Migration uses `git rm --cached` exclusively — never `git rm` plain
Pattern is tight (`tasks/progress-*.txt`), not overly broad
Failure modes (not-a-git-repo, no-tracked-files, dirty WT) all handled without panic
userStories.title: Gitignore tasks/progress-*.txt + untrack existing tracked progress files

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `ProjectConfig` exposes `pub slot_stash_limit: u32` with rustdoc explaining the policy
Positive: `default_slot_stash_limit()` returns 5
Positive: `ProjectConfig::default()` returns 5 for `slot_stash_limit`
Unit test: deserialize `{"version":1,"slotStashLimit":10}` produces `slot_stash_limit == 10`
Unit test: deserialize `{"version":1}` (field absent) produces `slot_stash_limit == 5`
Unit test: deserialize `{"version":1,"slotStashLimit":0}` produces `slot_stash_limit == 0` and is accepted
Negative: no other field in `ProjectConfig` is renamed, removed, or has its default changed
CONTRACT: field name in JSON is `slotStashLimit` (camelCase per `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` on the struct)
userStories.description:
Promote SLOT_STASH_LIMIT from a hardcoded const to a configurable field on `ProjectConfig` so operators can tune the bounded-warn-then-halt threshold without recompiling.

DO:
- Add `pub slot_stash_limit: u32` field to `ProjectConfig` in `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs`.
- Default to `5` via a `default_slot_stash_limit()` free function (mirror `default_merge_fail_halt_threshold()` at line ~89).
- Rustdoc explains: bounded warn-and-continue policy on stash-pop conflicts in `cleanup_preparation` (FEAT-003); when count of per-slot per-run stashes exceeds this, the slot is demoted to failed_slots(PreResolver) and the consecutive-merge-fail halt threshold trips. Document the `0` value: accepted as 'never halt' for consistency with merge_fail_halt_threshold == 0.
- Update the `impl Default for ProjectConfig` block (line ~100) to include the new field.
- Add unit tests in project_config.rs verifying deserialize from `{"slotStashLimit":10}`, default = 5 when absent, accepts 0.

DO NOT:
- Wire the field into `cleanup_preparation` in this task — FEAT-003 owns the integration.
- Promote any other unrelated const to config — single-purpose task.
- Add a CLI flag for it — operators set it via `.task-mgr/config.json` only.

Edge cases:
- Field absent in JSON → defaults to 5.
- Field set to 0 → accepted, interpreted as 'never halt' (matches `merge_fail_halt_threshold == 0`).
- Field set to very high value (e.g., 9999) → no special handling; operator accepts the risk.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Hard-coding the value in `cleanup_preparation` (FEAT-003) after this task — FEAT-003 must read from ProjectConfig and thread it through, not silently override.
edgeCases: Field absent in config JSON → defaults to 5
Field explicitly set to 0 → accepted, interpreted as 'unbounded'

failureModes.expectedBehavior: existing ProjectConfig error handling applies; this field doesn't introduce new failure modes

userStories.notes: ProjectConfig location: src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:11. Default function pattern at line 89 (`default_merge_fail_halt_threshold`). impl Default at line 100. Add field alongside `merge_fail_halt_threshold` since they're cousins (both control halt behavior). Tests likely live in the same file (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests` at the bottom) or in `tests/project_config.rs` — grep first.
qualityDimensions: Rustdoc explains policy clearly including the 0 special case
Defaults match plan: 5
Field added adjacent to merge_fail_halt_threshold, not appended at the end
userStories.title: Add slot_stash_limit to ProjectConfig (default 5)

acceptanceCriteria: Unit test: prepare_slot0_for_merge returns Clean when `git status --porcelain -uall` is empty
Unit test: prepare_slot0_for_merge with a dirty tracked file produces Stashed with tag matching `^task-mgr-slot-\d+-test-run-\d+$`
Unit test: prepare_slot0_for_merge with an untracked file (e.g., scratch.log) stashes it; post-prepare `git status --porcelain` is empty
Unit test: prepare_slot0_for_merge with BOTH tracked dirty file AND untracked file produces ONE stash entry (`git stash list | wc -l == 1`), not two
Unit test: cleanup_preparation(Clean, ...) is a no-op and returns Restored
Unit test: cleanup_preparation(Stashed{tag}, ...) after clean dirty-then-stash sequence pops the stash, restores the dirty file (post-cleanup `git status --porcelain` shows it), and returns Restored
Unit test: cleanup_preparation under forced pop conflict returns PopConflict with conflicted path; `git stash list` still shows the entry; working tree contains conflict markers
Unit test: cleanup_preparation when per-slot per-run stash count exceeds stash_limit returns StashLimitExceeded — pre-seed stash_limit synthetic stashes with matching tag prefix

Unit test: cleanup_preparation counts ONLY tags matching `task-mgr-slot-{slot}-{run_id}-*` — pre-seed stashes for a DIFFERENT slot index; verify cleanup does NOT report StashLimitExceeded (per-slot scoping)
Unit test: cleanup_preparation counts ONLY tags matching this run_id — pre-seed stashes for a different run_id; verify cleanup does NOT report StashLimitExceeded (per-run scoping)
Unit test: run_id 'test-run' produces deterministic tag prefixes across two consecutive prepare calls in the same test (epoch-millis suffix differs)
Negative: MergePreparation has exactly two variants (Clean, Stashed); CleanupOutcome has exactly three (Restored, PopConflict, StashLimitExceeded) — no Committed, no AutoResolved
Negative: no `chore(progress)` or `chore:` auto-commit appears in `git log` after any unit test
Negative: cleanup_preparation never invokes `git checkout --`, `git reset --hard`, or `git stash drop` (grep the implementation)
Failure mode: if `git stash push` exits non-zero, prepare_slot0_for_merge returns Err with formatted git stderr; working tree state is whatever git left it in
userStories.description:
Defense-in-depth for slot 0's merge-back: when slot 0 has uncommitted non-progress dirty state (build artifacts, log files, stray test fixtures), stash it before merge and pop it after. Progress files no longer enter this path (FEAT-001 gitignores them, so `git status --porcelain` doesn't see them), but other dirty state still can.

NO integration with merge_slot_branches_with_resolver in this task — that's FEAT-004. This delivers helpers + unit tests in isolation.

DO:
- Define `MergePreparation` enum: `Clean` and `Stashed { tag: String }`. (No `Committed` variant — auto-commit is forbidden per prohibitedOutcomes; the gitignore approach removes the only motivation for it.)
- Define `CleanupOutcome` enum: `Restored`, `PopConflict { tag: String, conflicted: Vec<String> }`, `StashLimitExceeded { count: usize }`.
- Implement `prepare_slot0_for_merge(slot0_path: &Path, slot: usize, run_id: &str) -> Result<MergePreparation, String>`. Runs `git status --porcelain -uall`. If empty → return Clean. Otherwise → `git stash push --include-untracked -m "task-mgr-slot-{slot}-{run_id}-{epoch_ms}"`; return Stashed { tag }.
- Implement `cleanup_preparation(prep: &MergePreparation, slot0_path: &Path, slot: usize, run_id: &str, stash_limit: u32) -> CleanupOutcome`. For Stashed: resolve stash ref by tag via `git stash list --format='%gd %gs'` + first-line tag match, then `git stash pop <ref>`. Clean pop → Restored. Pop conflict → count stashes matching `task-mgr-slot-{slot}-{run_id}-*`; if > stash_limit return StashLimitExceeded, else return PopConflict { tag, conflicted }.
- Stash limit count uses per-slot per-run prefix (NOT broad `task-mgr-slot-`) so concurrent loops / sibling slots don't trip each other.
- No regex crate; use `str::starts_with` matching on tag prefix.

DO NOT:
- Auto-commit anything. Stash everything (FEAT-001's gitignore means progress files don't reach this code).
- Classify dirty paths by file type. Single stash covers everything that git considers dirty.
- Touch merge_slot_branches_with_resolver in this task — wiring belongs to FEAT-004.
- Use git stash@{N} positional references — always resolve by message tag.
- Destroy user data on pop conflict (no `git checkout --`, `git reset --hard`, or `git stash drop`).

Edge cases:
- Empty working tree → Clean; no stash created. (With FEAT-001 in place, this is now the COMMON case — progress files no longer trigger dirtiness.)
- Stash push fails (e.g., submodule with uncommitted changes that --include-untracked can't capture) → Err returned, no partial state.
- Stash pop succeeds without conflict → Restored.
- Stash pop hits a conflict → leave stash on stack, report conflicted paths; do NOT auto-resolve.

Known-bad to guard against:
- `git stash list` parsing splitting on whitespace breaks on multi-word messages — use `--format='%gd %gs'` and split only on first space.
- A test that pushes an empty stash via `--allow-empty` and asserts the prepare path created one — empty stashes are NOT created by prepare; that would test a fake code path.
- Counting `task-mgr-slot-*` (any slot, any run) instead of `task-mgr-slot-{slot}-{run_id}-*` (the specific scope) would false-halt on sibling-loop activity.
edgeCases: Clean working tree → Clean, no stash
Stash push fails → Err, no partial state
Stash list parsing on multi-word messages → handled via --format='%gd %gs'
Pop conflict → leave stash, report conflicted paths
Sibling loop creates stashes with prefix `task-mgr-slot-` → not counted (different run_id)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: prepare returns Err with formatted stderr+stdout; caller logs and skips slot

failureModes.cause: git stash pop exits non-zero AND not a conflict
failureModes.expectedBehavior: cleanup returns PopConflict with conflicted=[] and the stash tag

failureModes.cause: git stash list parsing fails (corrupt output)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: cleanup returns Restored (best-effort); StashLimitExceeded will catch persistent issues later

userStories.notes: Place helpers in src/loop_engine/worktree.rs after is_worktree_dirty (~line 896). Reuse `format_git_failure` (~line 1564) for error message formatting. Reuse `setup_git_repo_with_file()` (src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs:245) in tests. Tag format: `task-mgr-slot-{slot}-{run_id}-{epoch_ms}`. Use `std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()` for epoch_ms. Learning [1806]: never destroy user data on cleanup — log, leave state inspectable, let operator decide.
qualityDimensions: All pub(crate) helpers have rustdoc explaining contract including what Err means
No regex crate; prefix matching via str::starts_with
Pop-conflict path NEVER destroys user data
Stash count check is scoped per-slot per-run, not global
Tag format is deterministic for a fixed run_id (epoch suffix is the only varying part)
userStories.title: Stash-based preflight helpers (prepare_slot0_for_merge + cleanup_preparation)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` signature is `(_project_root, branch_name, num_slots, resolver, slot_paths, run_id: &str, stash_limit: u32)`
Positive: engine.rs call site at ~line 1810 passes `params.run_id` and `params.project_config.slot_stash_limit`

Positive: `run_slot_merge_attempt(slot, slot0_path, ephemeral, resolver) -> Result<(), (String, SlotFailureKind)>` is a new private function in worktree.rs; the per-slot loop calls it exactly once per slot
Positive: cleanup_preparation is called exactly once per slot iteration AFTER run_slot_merge_attempt returns — verifiable by code reading
Positive: merge_resolver.rs:278 message ends with the appended hint (exact string match)
Unit test (test_merge_back_clean_wt_unchanged_behavior): clean slot 0 + disjoint slot-1 commit → merged_slots = [1], no chore commit in `git log --grep '^chore'`, no stash created
Unit test (test_merge_back_stashes_dirty_unrelated_file): dirty scratch.log → merge succeeds, post-merge `git status --porcelain` shows scratch.log, stash list empty

Unit test (test_merge_back_pop_conflict_keeps_stash_and_warns): slot-1's commit modifies scratch.log (same path as stashed) → merged_slots = [1], NOT in failed_slots, stash list retains entry with tag `task-mgr-slot-1-test-run-*`

Unit test (test_merge_back_excessive_stash_count_demotes_slot): pre-seed stash_limit stashes with matching tag prefix, dirty scratch.log + conflicting slot-1 commit → failed_slots contains slot with PreResolver kind
Unit test (test_merge_back_run_id_isolates_stash_tags): pre-seed stash_limit stashes for 'other-run' → slot does NOT demote (per-run scoping)

Unit test (test_merge_back_resolver_path_works_with_preflight): dirty scratch.log + conflicting merge that resolver can resolve → merged_slots contains slot, HEAD advanced, stash popped post-resolution
Negative: no test expects a chore commit on the base branch
Negative: removing the cleanup_preparation call from the post-attempt position causes at least one new test to fail
Negative: hardcoding `stash_limit = 5` inside cleanup_preparation (ignoring the parameter) causes the limit-exceeded test to behave incorrectly when stash_limit is set to a different value
CONTRACT: pre_merge_head captured inside run_slot_merge_attempt AFTER prepare_slot0_for_merge returns Ok
Failure mode: prepare returns Err → slot pushed to failed_slots(PreResolver) with 'pre-merge prep failed: {e}'; cleanup NOT called
Failure mode: run_slot_merge_attempt returns Err AND cleanup returns StashLimitExceeded → outcomes.failed_slots contains slot once; both diagnostics folded into message
userStories.description:
Wire FEAT-003's helpers into the per-slot loop body. Extract the per-slot body into `run_slot_merge_attempt` so cleanup runs on every exit path. Thread `run_id` and `stash_limit` through the function signature. Update the engine.rs call site. Apply 1-line diagnostic tweak in merge_resolver.rs.

DO:
- Change `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` signature to accept `run_id: &str` and `stash_limit: u32`. Update the engine.rs call site at line ~1810 (params.run_id already in scope; ProjectConfig cached in WaveIterationParams::project_config so `params.project_config.slot_stash_limit` is the threading source).
- Inside the per-slot loop body: call `prepare_slot0_for_merge(slot0_path, slot, run_id)` BEFORE the rev-parse + git merge. On Err, push to failed_slots(PreResolver), continue (no cleanup needed; prepare failed before side effect).
- Extract the post-prepare body (rev-parse + git merge + handle_conflict_for_slot) into private `run_slot_merge_attempt(slot, slot0_path, ephemeral, resolver) -> Result<(), (String, SlotFailureKind)>`. The helper captures its own `pre_merge_head` post-prepare. Outer loop: `let attempt_result = run_slot_merge_attempt(...)` then `let cleanup_result = cleanup_preparation(&prep, slot0_path, slot, run_id, stash_limit)`. ALWAYS run cleanup after run_slot_merge_attempt returns, regardless of attempt outcome.
- Fold cleanup into outcome: Restored or PopConflict leaves attempt's outcome as recorded; StashLimitExceeded demotes the slot to failed_slots(PreResolver) even if merge attempt succeeded.
- Emit a warning on PopConflict naming tag + conflicted paths; stronger warning on StashLimitExceeded.
- Apply 1-line diagnostic tweak in src/loop_engine/merge_resolver.rs:278: append ' (likely dirty WT blocked merge precondition; preflight should have prevented this — see worktree::prepare_slot0_for_merge; if this fires, check for non-gitignored dirty files in slot 0)' to the existing 'no conflicts reported, refusing to spawn' string.
- Update all existing tests calling merge_slot_branches_with_resolver to pass a test `run_id` (e.g., 'test-run') and a test `stash_limit` (use the default 5 or a smaller value for limit-exceeded tests). Find call sites via `grep -n 'merge_slot_branches_with_resolver(' src/loop_engine/worktree.rs`.
- Add 6 integration tests as per the plan (clean WT baseline; dirty unrelated file; pop conflict keeps stash; excessive stash count demotes; run_id isolation; resolver-clean and resolver-declined paths still work).

DO NOT:
- Re-introduce progress-file classification or any path-based filtering in this PRD's preflight code. FEAT-001's gitignore is the only path-based handling needed; progress files no longer reach this code path.
- Auto-commit anything. Forbidden.
- Move `pre_merge_head` capture BEFORE `prepare_slot0_for_merge`. Must be post-prepare.
- Skip cleanup on the rev-parse failure path. The `run_slot_merge_attempt` extraction makes 'cleanup runs once at end' a structural invariant.
- Use stash@{N} positional refs. Always resolve by tag.
- Change the meaning of `slot0_poisoned` — if poisoned, slot skips prepare AND cleanup.

Edge cases:
- Clean slot 0 + clean merge → merged_slots contains slot; no stash; no auto-commits anywhere.
- Dirty slot 0 + clean merge → stash, merge, pop cleanly; merged_slots contains slot.
- Dirty slot 0 + merge conflict (resolver succeeds) → stash, resolver resolves, HEAD advances, pop on top.
- Dirty slot 0 + merge conflict (resolver declines) → stash, hard_reset(pre_merge_head), pop onto reset HEAD.
- Dirty slot 0 + pop conflict (rare; only happens for non-gitignored files slot 1 also touched) → slot in merged_slots, stash retained, warning logged.
- Excessive stash accumulation → slot demoted to failed_slots(PreResolver).

Known-bad to guard against:
- Capturing `pre_merge_head` BEFORE prepare would create an inconsistent reset target.
- Calling cleanup INSIDE per-slot match arms would miss the rev-parse-failure path. Use the extraction.
- Threading `stash_limit` from anywhere other than ProjectConfig defeats FEAT-002.
edgeCases: Clean slot 0 + clean merge → Clean preparation, cleanup no-op
Dirty slot 0 + clean merge → stash, pop transparent
Dirty slot 0 + resolver-resolved conflict → stash applied AFTER resolver commits
Dirty slot 0 + resolver-declined conflict → hard_reset, then stash pop
Pop conflict → slot in merged_slots, stash retained
Excessive stash accumulation → slot demoted
Multi-run scoping → another run's stashes don't contribute to count

failureModes.expectedBehavior: slot in failed_slots(PreResolver) with prep error; cleanup not called; continue to next slot
warning logged with tag + conflicted paths; slot's attempt outcome preserved
slot demoted to failed_slots(PreResolver) regardless of attempt outcome; stronger warning with count

ProjectConfig cached in WaveIterationParams::project_config (per CLAUDE.md 'Run-level config caching'). Existing tests calling merge_slot_branches_with_resolver: lines 2639, 2724, 2820, 3100, 3154, 3196, 3237, 3288, 3311 (verify by re-grep — FEAT-003 may shift line numbers). All need test run_id + stash_limit. Data contract: ProjectConfig.slot_stash_limit (project_config.rs) → cleanup_preparation arg (worktree.rs); both u32.
qualityDimensions: Cleanup runs on EVERY post-prepare exit path — structural invariant via run_slot_merge_attempt extraction
stash_limit threaded from ProjectConfig, not hardcoded
Existing tests pass after adding new parameters (use 'test-run' run_id and default 5 stash_limit)
merge_resolver.rs diagnostic tweak is exactly 1 line of source text
No new public API surface — run_slot_merge_attempt is private
userStories.title: Integrate preflight into merge_slot_branches_with_resolver + diagnostic tweak

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots` signature adds `auto_recovery: Option<&AutoRecoveryConfig<'_>>` as the last parameter
Positive: when auto_recovery is None, the function behavior is byte-for-byte identical to today (regression test asserts same Err for same input)
Positive: when auto_recovery is Some and the stale ephemeral merges cleanly with preflight, the branch is deleted and reconcile returns Ok

Unit test (test_reconcile_auto_recovery_disabled_preserves_abort): unmerged ephemeral + auto_recovery=None + halt_threshold=2 → same InvalidState error as today

Unit test (test_reconcile_auto_recovery_succeeds_when_merge_clean): unmerged ephemeral with only a non-conflicting commit + auto_recovery=Some(mock_resolver) → branch deleted, reconcile Ok, project_root HEAD advanced
Unit test (test_reconcile_auto_recovery_fails_falls_through_to_abort): unmerged ephemeral with conflicting commit + mock resolver returns Failed → reconcile returns Err, abort message annotated

Unit test (test_reconcile_auto_recovery_handles_dirty_project_root): dirty scratch.log in project_root + auto_recovery=Some + clean-merge ephemeral → preflight stashes, merges, cleanup pops, reconcile Ok, scratch.log dirty again post-call
Unit test (test_reconcile_auto_recovery_mixed_outcomes): two stale ephemerals, one merges, one conflicts and resolver declines → first deleted; second in abort message; Err returned

CONTRACT: AutoRecoveryConfig fields match exactly the struct shown in description (model: &'a str, effort: &'a str, claude_timeout: Duration, signal_flag: Arc<AtomicBool>, db_dir: Option<&'a Path>, run_id: &'a str, stash_limit: u32)

userStories.description:
Extend `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots` to attempt automatic merge-back of stale unmerged ephemeral branches at loop startup, using FEAT-004's preflight + resolver path. Cleans up legacy strandings (created before this PRD's gitignore + preflight landed) without operator intervention.

DO:
- Define `AutoRecoveryConfig<'a>` struct in worktree.rs alongside MergePreparation/CleanupOutcome:
```rust
pub(crate) struct AutoRecoveryConfig<'a> {
    pub model: &'a str,
    pub effort: &'a str,
    pub claude_timeout: Duration,
    pub signal_flag: Arc<AtomicBool>,
    pub db_dir: Option<&'a Path>,
    pub run_id: &'a str,
    pub stash_limit: u32,
}
```
- Extend `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots` signature to add `auto_recovery: Option<&AutoRecoveryConfig<'_>>` as the trailing parameter. When None, behavior is byte-for-byte identical to today. When Some, attempt per-branch merge-back using `run_slot_merge_attempt` + `cleanup_preparation` with `slot0_path = project_root` (slot 0 IS the loop's main worktree at startup).
- On per-branch auto-recovery success: delete the now-merged ephemeral via `delete_branch_force` (worktree.rs:949), remove from `unmerged` before the abort decision.
- On per-branch auto-recovery failure: keep branch in `unmerged`, fall through to existing halt_threshold abort; surface in the abort message which branches the resolver attempted vs. didn't.
- Wire `auto_recovery: Some(&cfg)` from engine.rs startup (before `ensure_slot_worktrees`). Build a one-off ClaudeMergeResolver from `ProjectConfig.merge_resolver_model` / `merge_resolver_effort` / `merge_resolver_timeout_secs` (existing fields with defaults).
- Add tests: legacy abort path preserved when auto_recovery is None; successful auto-recovery deletes branch and lets startup continue; failed auto-recovery still aborts with annotated message; multi-branch mixed-outcome case; dirty project_root preflight stashes then pops.
- Update CLAUDE.md `Slot merge-back conflict resolution` AND/OR `Parallel-slot scheduling` section: ~1 paragraph each for (a) gitignore approach (FEAT-001), (b) preflight stash for residual dirty state (FEAT-003/004), (c) reconcile auto-recovery (this task). FEAT-005 owns the CLAUDE.md edit; REVIEW-001 only verifies.

DO NOT:
- Spawn the resolver from a separate worktree or fresh slot setup. Reconcile runs before slot worktrees exist; use project_root directly.
- Change halt_threshold semantics.
- Make auto_recovery mandatory. None must preserve today's behavior.
- Run auto-recovery on case-4 dirty-worktree branches. Dirty worktrees still abort as today.
- Try to fast-forward the stale ephemeral's slot worktree — it's gone (this is reconcile of stragglers).
- Broaden `classify_ephemeral_branch`'s slot-0 rejection guard — it's load-bearing per CLAUDE.md.

Edge cases:
- Zero stale ephemerals → early return Ok.
- Stale ephemeral + auto_recovery=Some + clean preflight + resolver-clean merge → branch auto-merged, deleted; Ok.
- Stale ephemeral + auto_recovery=Some + dirty project_root → preflight stashes; merge runs; cleanup pops; branch deleted on success.
- Stale ephemeral + auto_recovery=Some + resolver declines → preflight cleanup reverses; branch remains in unmerged; abort fires.
- Multiple stale ephemerals + mixed outcomes → successful ones deleted; failed ones surface in abort message.
- auto_recovery=None → byte-for-byte identical to today.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A test that asserts auto-recovery runs when None was passed would mask a regression.
- Mutating `unmerged` mid-iteration without preserving order. Build recovery outcomes first, THEN re-emit warnings.
- Bypassing the slot-0 guard in classify_ephemeral_branch.
edgeCases: auto_recovery None → identical to today
auto_recovery Some + clean merge → branch deleted, Ok
auto_recovery Some + dirty project_root + clean merge → preflight stash/pop, Ok
auto_recovery Some + resolver-declined → abort with annotated message
Multiple stale ephemerals mixed outcomes → partial success surfaced
Case-4 dirty stale worktree → unchanged abort behavior
spawn_claude missing → preflight cleanup, ephemeral stays in unmerged, abort

failureModes.expectedBehavior: preflight cleanup runs; ephemeral stays in unmerged; case-3 abort fires with annotated message
branch skipped; stays in unmerged; abort with prep-failure diagnostic
warning logged; branch remains in unmerged for abort decision

userStories.notes: reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots: src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:662. Engine startup call site: grep `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots(` in engine.rs. ClaudeMergeResolver constructor: src/loop_engine/merge_resolver.rs ~line 220. delete_branch_force: src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:949. Reuse run_slot_merge_attempt + cleanup_preparation from FEAT-004 verbatim — pass slot0_path = project_root. Out-of-scope: case-4 dirty worktree.
qualityDimensions: auto_recovery None path is bit-for-bit identical to today's function — verified by regression test
Auto-recovery reuses FEAT-004's helpers, not parallel re-implementation
Halt-threshold semantics unchanged when None or when recovery fails
slot-0 safety guard in classify_ephemeral_branch preserved
Operator-visible abort message includes 'auto-recovery attempted' framing

userStories.notes: If issues: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`. Use rust-python-code-reviewer agent for the review pass. Common smells in this PRD: copy-paste between gitignore marker-block code and worktree.rs attr marker-block; hardcoded `"task-mgr-slot-"` in multiple places; run_slot_merge_attempt growing to handle both wave-merge and reconcile-recovery without clean factoring.

Shared marker-block logic between .gitignore and info/attributes if structurally identical
